name: HKUST-1
description: Optimal for CH₄ and CO₂ storage. Paddle-wheel Cu nodes create high uptake capacity at moderate pressure. One of the most extensively studied benchmark MOFs.

name: UiO-66
description: Exceptional water and thermal stability up to 500°C. Top candidate for industrial catalysis and aqueous applications due to its robust Zr₆ oxo-cluster nodes.

name: UiO-67
description: Extended biphenyl linker version of UiO-66 with larger pores and higher surface area. Excellent for bulky molecule catalysis and high-pressure CO₂ capture.

name: MOF-5
description: Prototype benchmark MOF with cubic topology. Exceptional gravimetric hydrogen uptake at 77K. Sensitive to moisture; best suited for dry gas storage applications.

name: ZIF-8
description: Sodalite topology with 3.4 Å aperture. Highly selective for H₂/CO₂ separation and post-combustion capture. Exceptional chemical and thermal stability.

name: MIL-101(Cr)
description: Record-breaking BET surface area among Cr-MOFs. Mesoporous cages enable drug delivery and bulky molecule catalysis. Stable in aqueous and acidic media.

name: MIL-53(Al)
description: Flexible breathing MOF with pressure-responsive gate-opening. Ideal for CO₂/CH₄ separation at ambient conditions. Exhibits remarkable structural flexibility.

name: NU-1000
description: Hierarchical pore system with mesoporous channels ideal for enzyme immobilization and single-site catalysis. Exceptional stability for post-synthetic metalation.

name: MOF-808
description: Spiro-type topology with accessible Zr₆ nodes for Lewis-acid catalysis. Outstanding for nerve agent hydrolysis and Brønsted acid catalysis. High defect-site density.

name: DUT-49
description: Exhibits negative gas adsorption (NGA) — a unique structural contraction at threshold pressure releases previously adsorbed gas. Ideal for pressure-swing gas storage studies.

name: MIL-125(Ti)
description: Photoactive titanium nodes enable photocatalytic water splitting and organic transformation under visible light. Stable in aqueous media with cyclic photocatalytic performance.

name: MOF-177
description: Ultra-high surface area MOF. Exceptional gravimetric CO₂ uptake at 50 bar. BTB tricarboxylate linker creates a low-density framework ideal for high-pressure gas storage.

name: ZIF-67
description: Cobalt analogue of ZIF-8 with identical topology but enhanced catalytic activity. Excellent electrocatalytic performance for oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) and CO₂ reduction.

name: Cu-BTC
description: Isoreticular to HKUST-1 with open Cu² sites after activation. Strong Lewis acid sites for catalysis and gas binding. Widely used as industrial adsorbent for H₂S removal.

name: MOF-74(Mg)
description: Highest CO₂ uptake among benchmark MOFs at 1 bar and 298K due to dense open Mg² metal sites along helical rods. Optimal for post-combustion carbon capture.

name: UiO-66-NH₂
description: Amine-functionalized UiO-66 with enhanced CO₂ affinity through chemisorption. Photocatalytically active under visible light. Widely explored for drug loading and controlled release.

name: MOF-919
description: Highly selective CO₂ over CH₄ separation via cooperative amine binding. Bimodal pore distribution enables both molecular sieving and chemisorptive capture simultaneously.

name: PCN-222
description: Mesoporous MOF with porphyrin linkers acting as single-atom catalytic sites. Ideal for enzyme-mimicking peroxidase activity, photosensitization, and heavy metal detection.

name: MOF-867
description: Bipyridyl linker coordinates post-synthetically to metal ions enabling single-atom catalysis. Excellent platform for Ir, Ru, and Co metalation for photocatalytic H₂ evolution.

name: MOF-801
description: Leading MOF for atmospheric water harvesting in arid climates. Steep water adsorption isotherm between 10–30% relative humidity enables passive water collection from desert air.

name: MOF-841
description: Second-generation water harvesting MOF with superior gravimetric water uptake over MOF-801. Highly porous Zr-framework capturing 0.25 g H₂O / g MOF at low humidity.

name: MIL-100(Fe)
description: Iron-based MIL-100 is biocompatible, FDA-relevant, and leading candidate for drug encapsulation. Giant cages accommodate large drug molecules including anticancer agents busulfan and camptothecin.

name: PCN-250
description: High volumetric methane storage MOF exceeding the 2025 DOE target. Mixed-valent Fe₂Fe trimer nodes provide stability in humid conditions and selective CO₂ adsorption.

name: NU-125
description: Among the best MOFs for volumetric methane storage exceeding 263 v/v at 65 bar. Silane-based core linker creates an ultra-low-density framework with exceptional gravimetric uptake.

name: BAF-1
description: Zeolite imidazolate framework with ultra-narrow 3.1 Å pores. Exceptional selectivity for H₂ over all other gases. Rigid framework ideal for kinetic molecular sieving applications.

name: IRMOF-3
description: Amine-functionalized IRMOF with tunable post-synthetic modification sites. Linker-NH₂ groups enable Schiff base reactions for catalytic applications and CO₂ chemisorption enhancement.

name: MOF-199
description: Commercial-scale Cu paddlewheel MOF widely used as Basolite C300. Used industrially for natural gas purification and selective H₂S/SO₂ removal. Low-cost scalable synthesis.

name: PCN-14
description: Anthracene-containing linker provides hydrophobic inner pore surface ideal for natural gas storage. Holds records for absolute methane uptake at 290K and 35 bar conditions.

name: MOF-303
description: State-of-the-art MOF for atmospheric water harvesting in sub-10% RH desert conditions. Al-rod MOF with ultra-steep water isotherms demonstrated in field trials for autonomous water generation.

name: MOF-520
description: Al-based MOF with hierarchical pore system enabling precise crystallographic quantification of pharmaceuticals and pesticides. Used as a single-crystal X-ray diffraction trap for guest molecules.
